differences.note: Production is a full-page centered composition; prototype is a right-docked flyout panel over a darkened background.
Production uses larger vertical gaps and roomy blocks; prototype is denser with tighter paddings and more compact section rhythm.
Production typography is larger and less compact; prototype uses a tighter type scale with clearer small-label/body/header hierarchy.
Production accents are brighter (cyan/yellow) and higher saturation; prototype uses more muted slate/teal tones with controlled accent usage.
Production corners are generally larger and more uniform; prototype uses more varied, slightly tighter radii across nested cards.
Prototype has stronger layered depth (glass/blur + soft shadows); production appears flatter with lighter shadow treatment.
Production hero/logo and avatar proportions are larger relative to container; prototype constrains imagery more tightly within layout modules.
Prototype has deeper module hierarchy (many compact sections); production has fewer modules and flatter information structure.

top_3_fixes: Switch to a right-side flyout container with fixed width, dark backdrop overlay, and panel-based structure to match the prototype’s primary layout.
Adopt prototype spacing and typography scales (smaller body text, tighter paddings, denser section rhythm) to align information density and hierarchy.
Standardize visual tokens to prototype values: muted color palette, tighter/varied border radii, stronger layered shadows/blur, and constrained image/avatar sizing.
